title: nyctaginaceae
intro: The Nyctaginaceae rank, also known as the Four O'Clock Family, is a taxonomic family of flowering plants. It is a small family consisting of around 31 genera and approximately 550 species, primarily found in the Americas, Africa, and Australia. The plants within this family are often characterized by their showy flowers and distinctive seed pods. Nyctaginaceae species can be annuals or perennials and typically thrive in warm, dry environments.
